21.correct: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment (1992) deals with Panchayati Raj, giving constitutional status to rural local self-government.

wrong.A: Anti-defection is in the 52nd Amendment (Tenth Schedule).
wrong.C: Tribunals were added by the 42nd Amendment (Articles 323A/323B).
wrong.D: Cooperative societies were addressed by the 97th Amendment.

21.fact: The 74th Amendment (1992) similarly gave constitutional status to urban local bodies (municipalities).

22.correct: Sarojini Naidu was the first woman Governor of an Indian state (United Provinces, now Uttar Pradesh).

wrong.B: Indira Gandhi was India's first woman Prime Minister, not the first woman Governor.
wrong.C: Sucheta Kripalani was the first woman Chief Minister (of UP).
wrong.D: Vijayalakshmi Pandit was a diplomat and the first woman President of the UN General Assembly.

22.fact: Sarojini Naidu, the 'Nightingale of India', was also the first Indian woman to be President of the Indian National Congress.

23.correct: India's National Air Quality Index measures 8 pollutants: PM2.5, PM10, NO2, SO2, CO, O3, NH3 and Pb.

wrong.D: 10 is more than the number used.

23.fact: The AQI was launched in India in 2015 under the 'National Air Quality Index' initiative.

24.correct: In the Happiness Index, 'freedom to make life choices' refers to people's perception that they have the freedom/control to make their own life decisions.

wrong.A: It is not about freedom of the media.
wrong.C: It is not about economic liberalization.
wrong.D: It is broader than formal political democracy; it is about personal life choices.

24.fact: It is one of the six factors used in the World Happiness Report.

25.correct: Goa hosted the 37th National Games of India in 2023.

wrong.A: Maharashtra did not host the 37th National Games.

25.fact: The National Games are India's premier multi-sport domestic event.

26.correct: By 1990, India's external debt had become very high, contributing to the 1991 balance-of-payments crisis.

wrong.C: It was not manageable; it precipitated a crisis.

26.fact: In 1991 India had to pledge gold reserves and seek IMF assistance, triggering economic reforms.

27.correct: The British Salt Law was criticised because it gave the government a monopoly over salt production and imposed heavy taxes on this essential commodity.

wrong.B: It suppressed, rather than encouraged, private salt industries.
wrong.C: It denied, not allowed, Indians free access to salt.
wrong.D: It did not abolish traditional methods to benefit Indians; it monopolised salt.

27.fact: Gandhi's 1930 Dandi Salt March was a direct protest against the salt tax.

28.correct: The Alvar saints of South India were devoted to Lord Vishnu and composed devotional hymns in Tamil.

wrong.A: Devotion to Durga is not associated with the Alvars.
wrong.C: Agni is a Vedic deity, not the Alvars' focus.
wrong.D: Indra is a Vedic deity, not worshipped by the Alvars.

28.fact: The Nayanars were the corresponding Shaiva (Shiva-devotee) saints of the same Bhakti tradition.

29.correct: The Dharti Aaba Janjatiya Gram Utkarsh Abhiyan targets the development of about 63,843 tribal villages.

wrong.B: 78,569 is not the targeted number of villages.

29.fact: The scheme aims to improve infrastructure and services in tribal-majority villages.

30.correct: In the Vedic framework of the four 'purusharthas', Dharma emphasises ethical and righteous living.

wrong.A: Artha concerns wealth and material prosperity.
wrong.D: Moksha is liberation, the ultimate spiritual goal.

30.fact: The four purusharthas are Dharma, Artha, Kama and Moksha.

31.correct: Writing is a voluntary action - it is performed consciously under the control of the will.

wrong.D: Breathing is largely involuntary (though it can be briefly controlled).

31.fact: Voluntary actions are controlled by the cerebrum (the conscious part of the brain).

32.correct: Under Article 262, Parliament may by law provide for the adjudication of disputes over the use, distribution or control of inter-State river waters.

wrong.B: The President does not legislate on this.
wrong.C: The Council of Ministers does not provide for such adjudication by itself.
wrong.D: The Union Home Minister has no such specific power.

32.fact: Parliament enacted the Inter-State River Water Disputes Act, 1956 under this provision.

33.correct: The 'Losar' festival is celebrated to mark the Tibetan New Year, observed by Buddhist communities (e.g. in Ladakh, Sikkim, Arunachal).

wrong.A: It is not the Hindu New Year.
wrong.B: It is not the Tamil New Year (Puthandu).

33.fact: Losar involves prayers, masked dances and family feasts to welcome the new year.

34.correct: The Government of India launched the 5th Marine Census at the 'Coastal State Fisheries Conference 2025' held in Mumbai.

wrong.A: It was not launched in Kolkata.
wrong.B: It was not launched in Cuttack.
wrong.C: It was not launched in Chennai.

34.fact: A marine fisheries census counts fishing households, craft and infrastructure along the coast.

35.correct: The Office of the Registrar General and Census Commissioner of India comes under the Ministry of Home Affairs.

wrong.B: It is not under the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation.
wrong.C: It is not under the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment.
wrong.D: It is not under the (erstwhile) Ministry of Human Resource Development.

35.fact: This office conducts the decennial Census and maintains the National Population Register.

36.correct: A fire extinguisher works by cutting off the air (oxygen) supply, reducing the temperature of the fuel, or both - removing the conditions needed for combustion.

wrong.A: It does not add fuel to the fire.
wrong.B: It does not raise the fuel's temperature.
wrong.C: It does not increase the air supply.

36.fact: The 'fire triangle' needs fuel, heat and oxygen; removing any one puts out the fire.

37.correct: Moral suasion is a qualitative (selective) tool of the RBI, not a quantitative one - so it is NOT a quantitative tool.

wrong.A: The Cash Reserve Ratio is a quantitative tool.
wrong.B: The Bank Rate is a quantitative tool.
wrong.C: Open Market Operations are a quantitative tool.

37.fact: Quantitative tools affect the overall money supply; qualitative tools (like moral suasion) target specific sectors or behaviour.

38.correct: The 11th BRICS Parliamentary Forum was held in Brasilia (Brazil) in June 2025, as Brazil chaired BRICS that year.

wrong.A: Beijing did not host the 2025 forum.
wrong.D: Vladivostok was not the host city.

38.fact: Brazil held the BRICS chairship in 2025, hosting various BRICS meetings.

39.correct: 'A Shot at History: My Obsessive Journey to Olympic Gold' is the autobiography of shooter Abhinav Bindra.

wrong.A: Neeraj Chopra is a javelin thrower; this is not his book.
wrong.B: Gagan Narang is a shooter but not the author of this title.
wrong.D: Rajyavardhan Singh Rathore is a shooter-politician, not the author.

39.fact: Abhinav Bindra won India's first individual Olympic gold (10 m air rifle) at Beijing 2008.

40.correct: Until the Paris 2024 Olympics, India's highest medal tally in a single Olympics was at Tokyo 2020, with 7 medals.

wrong.B: 1932 was an early Games where India won only its hockey gold.

40.fact: Tokyo 2020 gave India 1 gold, 2 silver and 4 bronze - its best Olympic performance.
